session.assistant: I'll load and follow the skill instructions from the pr-feedback-classifier skill, then execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill with the specified arguments `--pr 9097`:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch PR #9097 feedback with all comments

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: TRUNK=$(erk exec detect-trunk-branch | jq -r '.trunk_branch') && git diff --stat -M -C "$TRUNK"...HEAD
Get file-level restructuring context for PR

**Branch:** `plnd/move-fakes-to-tests-03-09-1534`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9097#issuecomment-4026262526" }, { "id": 4026654393, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-09 13:20:04 PT

No violations detected. This PR consists of clean refactoring that moves test-only code from production packages to the test directory structure.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL pattern - Compliant (uses condition checks where appropriate)
✅ Pathlib usage - Compliant (uses Path throughout)
✅ Import organization - Compliant (absolute imports, no re-exports of test code)
✅ Property O(1) complexity - Compliant
✅ Indentation depth - Compliant (max 4 levels maintained)
✅ No test code in production - Compliant (test-only code properly isolated)

### Files Reviewed (Production Code Only)
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py`: Updated test examples and import path - Compliant
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py`: Removed test-only factory function - Compliant
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`: Removed test classmethod - Compliant
- `src/erk/core/context.py`: Clean re-exports and delegation - Compliant
- `src/erk/core/shell.py`: Clean re-exports - Compliant
- `src/erk/core/completion.py`: Clean re-exports - Compliant

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-09 13:20:04 PT**: No violations detected - clean refactoring moving test code from production packages to test directory structure

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9097#issuecomment-4026654393"
... [30 lines omitted] ...
"body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-09 13:20:30 PT

No violations found. This PR consists primarily of file movement and import refactoring.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) — No exceptions used for control flow
✅ Exception handling — Proper error boundaries at CLI level
✅ Path operations — All `.exists()` checks before `.resolve()`
✅ Import organization — Absolute imports, no gratuitous aliasing
✅ No re-exports — Empty `__init__.py`, canonical import paths
✅ Default parameters — Correctly restricted to test files only
✅ Keyword-only arguments — Proper markers for 5+ parameter functions
✅ Subprocess handling — No direct subprocess.run() calls reviewed

### Code Changes Summary
- **tests/test_utils/test_context.py** (NEW, 385 lines added): Test context factory functions moved from production code
 - `minimal_context()` and `context_for_test()` now in test suite
 - Default parameters are allowed in test files per dignified-python standards
 - LBYL pattern followed throughout with `if X is None:` checks
 
- **tests/test_utils/context_builders.py** (28 lines modified): New helper `real_github_for_test()` replaces removed classmethod
 - Default parameters allowed in test utilities
 
- **Removals** (356+ lines deleted from production code):
 - Removed `context_for_test()` and `minimal_context()` from `src/erk/core/context.py`
 - Removed test factory from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py`
 - Removed `RealLocalGitHub.for_test()` classmethod from production code

- **Import refactoring** (~460 files): Updated import statements from `erk_shared.fakes.*` → `tests/fakes/gateway/` and `tests/fakes/tests/`

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/core/context.py`: 0 violations
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py`: 0 violations
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/test_utils/test_context.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`: 0 violations
- Import-only changes: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-09 13:20:30 PT**: All violations resolved. No new violations detected in this PR.

</details>",

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed to classify the PR feedback. Let me analyze the data according to the skill instructions:
Based on the PR feedback data I've fetched and the file-level restructuring context, here's my analysis:
**PR Analysis:** - PR #9097: "Move fakes from production packages to tests/fakes/" - This is a significant refactoring that moves test-only code from production packages to test directories - File restructuring detected: Multiple `fake.py` files moved/renamed to `tests/fakes/gateway/`
**Comments Classification:**
1. **Discussion comments** (7 total): - schrockn: "Plan Queued for Implementation" - Informational (erk metadata) - github-actions[bot] x2: "GitHub Action Started" & "Starting implementation" - Informational (workflow status) - github-actions[bot] x4: "Dignified Code Simplifier Review", "Tripwires Review", "Dignified Python Review", "Test Coverage Review" - All Informational (CI status, no violations found) - github-actions[bot]: "Audit PR Docs" - This is **Actionable** (violations detected)
**Review threads:** None
The only actionable item is from the "Audit PR Docs" review which found 5 violations in documentation files regarding incorrect import paths introduced by this PR.
